entities.name: Star Petroleum Refining Public Company Limited

financial.entity: Chevron South Asia Holdings Pte. Ltd.
financial.note: Holds 60.56% stake; provides technical support and secures crude supply, particularly during geopolitical supply chain disruptions.
Holds ~10.55% stake; reflects foreign institutional flow and sentiment toward Thai energy refining spreads.
Holds ~1.52% stake; represents government-linked investment stability in a core national infrastructure asset.

supply_chain.note: Secures crude supply through 2Q26; helped SPRC reduce Middle East crude reliance from ~80% to <20% to mitigate geopolitical risks.
Major off-takers of refined products (diesel, jet fuel, gasoline) for the domestic Thai market.
175,000 bpd capacity (13.2% of Thailand's total); high Nelson Complexity Index of 6.3 allows for higher-margin product yields.

competitors.note: Primary domestic rival in complex refining; both compete for market share in middle distillates.
Rivalry intensified following BCP's acquisition of Esso Thailand; competes directly in retail and fuel marketing.
Competes in the refining segment, though IRPC has a heavier focus on integrated petrochemicals.

macro_commodity_factors.logic: The most critical earnings driver; every $1/bbl change impacts annual core earnings by ~฿700m–฿900m.
Drives crude price volatility, leading to massive inventory gains (est. ฿7.2b in 1Q26) despite core operational shutdowns.
Refining margins are denominated in USD; a weaker THB inflates translated earnings but can lead to FX losses on USD debt.
Middle distillates are SPRC's bread and butter; 1H26 tourism recovery in Thailand boosts domestic jet fuel demand.
